result.stdout:
quickup 1.2.0
Copyright (c) 2018 Sergio Pedri

  -i, --include       The list of file extensions to look for when scanning the
                      source directory. If not specified, all existing files 
                      will be copied.

  -e, --exclude       The list of optional file extensions to ignore.

  --ignore-dir        The list of optional subdirectories to ignore. Note that 
                      the dir name will be matched, not the relative path.

  -p, --preset        (Default: None) An optional preset to quickly filter 
                      certain common file types. This option cannot be used 
                      when --include or --exclude are used. Existing options 
                      are [documents|images|music|videos|code|vs].

  --id                An optional id to keep multiple backups of the same 
                      source folder in the same directory.

  -M, --maxsize       (Default: 104857600) The maximum size of files to be 
                      copied.

  -s, --source        The source directory to backup.

  --source-current    (Default: false) Shortcut to set the source directory as 
                      the current working directory.

  -t, --target        Required. The target directory to use to store the 
                      backup.

  -b, --beep          (Default: false) Play a sound when the requested 
                      operation completes.

  -v, --verbose       (Default: false) Indicates whether or not to display 
                      additional statistics.

  --multithread       (Default: false) Indicates whether or not to copy the 
                      files in parallel. This should be enabled when working on
                      an SSD.

  --threads           (Default: -1) Indicates the maximum number of threads to 
                      use to copy the files. If set to -1, either 1 or the 
                      number of CPU threads will be used.

  --help              Display this help screen.

  --version           Display version information.

options.description: The target directory to use to store the backup.
Play a sound when the requested operation completes.
The list of optional file extensions to ignore.
An optional id to keep multiple backups of the same source folder in the same directory.
The list of optional subdirectories to ignore. Note that the dir name will be matched, not the relative path.
The list of file extensions to look for when scanning the source directory. If not specified, all existing files will be copied.
The maximum size of files to be copied.
Indicates whether or not to copy the files in parallel. This should be enabled when working on an SSD.
An optional preset to quickly filter certain common file types. This option cannot be used when --include or --exclude are used. Existing options are [documents|images|music|videos|code|vs].
Shortcut to set the source directory as the current working directory.
Indicates the maximum number of threads to use to copy the files. If set to -1, either 1 or the number of CPU threads will be used.
Indicates whether or not to display additional statistics.
